source.title: Experience sampling during fMRI reveals default network and executive system contributions to mind wandering
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: PNAS paper with experience-sampling fMRI is well-executed but mapping to REE is indirect. The load-bearing finding for REE is the co-activation of default network and executive / rostral PFC regions during mind-wandering, which bears on MECH-261's internal_replay / offline_consolidation modes. The meta-awareness dissociation (higher activity when mind-wandering is unaware) is a secondary finding that is more speculative in the REE translation.
failure_signatures: If mind-wandering had recruited default network regions without executive/rostral PFC co-activation, the 'internal cognition engages both DMN and executive control' framing would collapse and REE's assumption that internal modes can use the same substrates as external_task mode (with different gating) would lose support.
If meta-awareness had suppressed rather than recruited frontopolar activity, the framing of BA 10 as a substrate for reflective / meta-level cognition would weaken.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Mind-wandering — spontaneous self-generated thought during an ongoing external task — co-activates default network regions (medial PFC, posterior cingulate) and executive network regions including rostral PFC. Activation is stronger when participants are unaware of their own mind-wandering than when they self-report it. This contradicts earlier framings in which default-mode and executive networks were taken to be anti-correlated, showing that internally-generated cognition engages both systems.
mapping.ree_translation: Provides fMRI grounding for MECH-261's internal modes (internal_planning, internal_replay) as states in which executive substrates including the frontopolar-analog (SD-033e) remain engaged — they are not offline just because the agent is not externally task-directed. The meta-awareness dissociation additionally hints at a function for the frontopolar-analog in meta-level monitoring of internal cognition: the agent can be generating internal content without being aware of it, and awareness (when present) correlates with rostral PFC engagement. That is a specific capability V4 will eventually need — a reflective layer that knows which mode the agent is in.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper does not isolate BA 10 from broader rostral PFC and does not dissociate frontopolar contributions from anterior cingulate or medial PFC contributions cleanly. The meta-awareness finding is suggestive but based on subjective report during a boring task, so the mapping to 'REE meta-cognitive reflection' is an extrapolation. The paper's main contribution to this lit-pull is the co-activation finding (DMN + executive during internal cognition) rather than a precise frontopolar function.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI with experience-sampling probes during a sustained attention to response task (SART); N=19; unpublished subjective reports of mind-wandering used as within-subject regressors.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality solid (PNAS, Christoff/Schooler labs, well-cited foundational paper for mind-wandering fMRI). Mapping fidelity moderate — the paper does not isolate frontopolar function cleanly; it is useful as corroboration that internal modes engage executive substrates, not as a direct test of SD-033e. Transfer risk higher — meta-awareness is a subjective construct, and SART-paradigm mind-wandering is a narrow slice of internal cognition.
